intro: Colona blancoi, also known as the Philippine colona, is a medicinal plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Colona blancoi include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Filipino medicine, Colona blancoi is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their ability to promote healing and boost the immune system.
